what_it_does: It is a living adversarial completion-refuter checklist that tracks open 'holes' and an L1–L5 closure sequence for a build, requiring each capability to be verified at every layer through to Tim's actual use before it can be considered closed.
observations: Adopts a 'default-to-wrong' adversarial stance; 'done' must be re-checked at backend, surface-call-site, and Tim-use layers.

Tracks three holes: HOLE-1 canonicalization at the chokepoint (fixed/committed), HOLE-3 grounded backend not used by surface calls (open), HOLE-2 grounding trace versus stored origin-field (routed/unverified).
Defines an L1–L5 Tim-use closure sequence from RHM walking Tim through grounded decisions up to RHM updating a decision card.
Each hole has state, re-check trigger, and layer-status breakdown.
References specific code locations and routes: runtime/territory.py, RightHand.tsx lines 201/562, /api/territory/write, /api/claude/turn, /api/brain/ask.
Updated 2026-06-21 by wildcard with ch work ids.
Floor states none are closed by the Tim-use bar yet.
standouts: The unifying diagnostic: a hole is a capability real at one layer but silently generalized to the whole loop.
HOLE-3 is explicitly open: the grounded backend route exists but RightHand.tsx still calls the generic .ask().
The closure bar is Tim's actual use, not merely backend existence or wire presence.
conventions: Follows a structured hole/closure-tracker format with state, re-check trigger, and layer status.
Uses adversarial QA convention: default-to-wrong, re-check on every landing.
Breaks conventional numeric ordering: HOLE-1, HOLE-3, HOLE-2.
Uses project-specific shorthand (Tim, RHM, V, popcorn-B, ch ids) without defining them in the file.
concerns: Some states are self-certified without evidence shown in the file (e.g., HOLE-1 'by-use verified' with no test output).
Heavy reliance on external owners/projection to close holes; the tracker cannot close them itself.
Undefined jargon may make the file opaque to new readers.
Closure criteria are qualitative ('by-sight', 'adds beyond the card') and may be inconsistently applied.
notes: An adversarial closure tracker that refuses to mark work done until it has been verified end-to-end at Tim's actual point of use.
questions: Who or what are Tim, RHM, V, popcorn-B, and the lead?
What is the exact definition of a 'grounded' versus 'generic' answer?
What is the per-decision retrieval-keying fix referenced for HOLE-2?
What is the decide-rung single-source mechanism and the noticeboard signal-write?
What do the ch ids (ch-869a5yzl / ch-piffgfxv) refer to?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a living completion-refuter checklist; the file does exactly that by listing holes, closure layers, re-check triggers, and current statuses, so purpose and actual align.
intention_signals: It tries to prevent premature 'done' claims by forcing every feature to be proven through backend, frontend, and real user-use layers, with a named adversarial role continuously re-checking.
novelty: The explicit 'default-to-wrong' adversarial kernel role and the L1–L5 Tim-use closure bar that treats backend existence as insufficient.
contribution: It contributes a structured, skeptical checkpoint format that ties technical holes to user-visible acceptance criteria and re-check triggers, which a fused system could keep as a live verification ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A living adversarial closure tracker for a software build that records open holes and an L1–L5 end-to-end verification sequence, requiring each capability to be proven through backend, surface call-site, and Tim's actual use.
intention_for_embedding: To act as a persistent completion-refuter that flags capabilities claimed as done at one layer but not yet verified all the way to Tim's use.
